title: Thunderclap headache in a 45-year-old female
presentation: A 45-year-old female with no significant past medical history presents with the worst headache of her life that began suddenly while exercising 3 hours ago. She describes it as an explosion in her head. She vomited twice. Vitals: BP 178/102, HR 88, RR 16, Temp 37.0C, SpO2 99%. Neuro exam: mild neck stiffness, cranial nerves intact, no focal deficits.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Thunderclap headache = SAH until proven otherwise. Negative CT does not rule out SAH — LP for xanthochromia required
